news.headline: Hong Kong-based Jardine Matheson buys Australian diagnostic group I-MED for US$2.4 billion
news.summary:
Jardine Matheson, an investment company headquartered in Hong Kong, announced on Monday the A$3.4 billion (US$2.4 billion) acquisition of the entire stake of an Australian diagnostic and teleradiology group.

news.headline: What are the biggest FIFA World Cup upsets in history?
news.summary: Nothing makes the news like an unfancied or low-ranked team pulling off a shock result at the football World Cup.

news.headline: Three killed in Uganda after crashing into elephant
news.summary: Four people were also injured in the incident inside Murchison Falls National Park, officials say.
